name: Idle Income
description: A recurring resource grant that accrues while the player waits, plays, upgrades generators, or returns after time away.
design_notes: Idle income shifts the economy from event rewards to rate management. The important boundaries are accrual rate, offline cap, claim transaction, generator upgrades, and anti-clock-tamper rules; without caps it can erase every other source and sink.
design_purpose: Reward long-term investment in generators, buildings, or account upgrades.
Create return-session momentum without requiring constant manual grinding.
Give designers a predictable currency source that can be balanced against upgrade costs and sinks.
player_fantasy: The player feels their economy keeps working for them while they plan the next investment.

parameters.name: generator_id
parameters.description: Identifier for the income source whose level, state, and ownership determine earnings.

parameters.name: income_rate_per_minute
parameters.description: Currency or resource amount generated by this source per minute before modifiers.

parameters.name: offline_accrual_cap_minutes
parameters.description: Maximum away-time credited during an offline claim.

parameters.name: claim_cooldown_seconds
parameters.description: Minimum delay between claim transactions for the same generator or account.

parameters.name: clock_tamper_grace_seconds
parameters.description: Tolerance before suspicious local time changes are ignored or require server time.

edge_cases: The player changes device time or resumes from sleep with a negative or enormous elapsed interval.
A generator is upgraded, sold, or disabled while unclaimed income exists.
Offline income reaches cap before the player returns, so further time should not inflate the wallet.
Multiple devices claim the same idle interval unless last_claim_timestamp is authoritative and synchronized.
common_bugs: Income uses frame delta while the app is backgrounded and grants a burst after resume without cap checks.
Upgrading a generator retroactively applies the new income_rate_per_minute to all previous offline time.
Claim UI shows projected income but the committed transaction uses a different elapsed time source.
Rounding per tick loses small fractional income forever instead of accumulating remainder state.
balancing_notes: Idle income should support, not replace, active play unless the whole game is built around idle progression.
Offline caps and generator upgrade costs control return-session pacing more than the raw rate alone.
Track idle source totals separately from active rewards to detect when passive grants dominate the economy.
accessibility_notes: Let players claim income through clear buttons and summaries rather than tiny rapidly changing counters.
Avoid requiring frequent timed check-ins unless reminders, batching, or accessibility-friendly schedules exist.

implementation_notes.unity: Persist last_claim_timestamp, generator_id levels, fractional_remainder, and claimed transaction_id; compute elapsed income when opening the wallet UI, then commit through the same currency service as other grants.
implementation_notes.godot: Use an Autoload income manager that reads generator Resources and a trusted time source; claim_income should clamp elapsed minutes and write the new timestamp before granting currency.
implementation_notes.unreal: For networked games, calculate idle income on the server or backend using account timestamps; clients should only request a claim and display the committed result.
implementation_notes.web: Use Date.now only for local prototypes; production browser games should verify elapsed time server-side and persist generator state plus last_claim_timestamp with the account save.

example_games.title: Cookie Clicker
example_games.note: Uses generators that produce currency over time.

example_games.title: AdVenture Capitalist
example_games.note: Uses business upgrades and offline earnings as core progression.
